hibernate/src/main/java/com/example/Main.general_idea: Register new entity classes (Laptop, Passport, ClassRoom) in the Hibernate configuration to support the expanded data model.
Add logic to create and persist Laptop entities before creating the Student to handle the one-to-many relationship.
Add logic to create and persist a Passport entity to support the Student's one-to-one relationship.
Add logic to create and persist a ClassRoom entity using a builder pattern to support the Student's many-to-many relationship.
Replace the simple Student constructor with a builder pattern to initialize all new relationships (laptops, passport, classrooms).
Implement a read operation to load the persisted Student from the database and verify the data.
Implement an update operation to modify the loaded Student's name and merge changes back to the database.
Implement a delete operation block for the Student entity, although the actual removal call is commented out.
Add explicit closing of the Session and SessionFactory to ensure proper resource management.

hibernate/src/main/java/com/example/Stud.general_idea: Replace manual getters, setters, and constructors with Lombok @Data, @AllArgsConstructor, @NoArgsConstructor, and @Builder annotations to reduce boilerplate.
Add @GeneratedValue(strategy = GenerationType.IDENTITY) to the ID field to explicitly define the primary key generation strategy.
Add a OneToOne relationship field for Passport to model the association between Student and Passport.
Add a OneToMany relationship field for a list of Laptops to model the association between Student and Laptops.
Add a ManyToMany relationship field for a list of ClassRooms to model the association between Student and ClassRooms.

hibernate/pom.xml.general_idea: Add Project Lombok as a compile-time dependency to reduce boilerplate code.

hibernate/src/main/resources/hibernate.c.general_idea: Switched Hibernate schema generation from update to create to ensure a fresh database schema on startup.
Enabled SQL statement logging to console for debugging purposes.
Enabled pretty-printing of SQL statements in logs for better readability.
Explicitly configured the PostgreSQL dialect to ensure correct SQL generation.

hibernate/target/classes/hibernate.cfg.x.general_idea: Switch Hibernate schema generation from update to create to reset the database schema on startup.
Enable SQL statement logging for debugging purposes.
Enable formatted SQL output for better readability in logs.
Explicitly configure the PostgreSQL dialect for Hibernate.
